page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across multiple metrics. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1876.98 and a multi-core score of 15440.75. Memory performance tests show a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 831.5 GB/sec, which drops to 84.73 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 54.87 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the CPU supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, delivering a PassMark CPU score of 19226.97 and a database operations score of 7793.61.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. However, the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, and the absence of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning tasks. It is suited for web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database operations, as demonstrated by its static web serving throughput of 16.69 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 15.82 million operations per second.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this KVM-hypervised instance relies on a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate steady mid-tier performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15440.75 and a PassMark CPU score of 19226.97. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive tasks that do not demand massive memory allocations. It is well-positioned for workloads such as static web serving, which reaches a throughput of 16.69 GB/sec, multi-threaded decompression, and Redis database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-range performance for web serving and database tasks.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute instance with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated sixteen-core compute instance optimized for processing-heavy workloads requiring moderate memory allocation and standard network throughput.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets for vector calculations
Delivers 15.82 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database workloads
